uk: Преподобного Зотика, пресвітера і кормителя сиріт.
saints.en: St. Martyr Anysia. Ven. Zoticus, Presbyter and Feeder of Orphans

rubrics:
Вечірня: на “Господи, взиваю” 6 стихир: 3 мчц. Анісії, 3 преподобної Меланії (з огляду на віддання Різдва), Сл., І нині: свята. Стиховня свята. По “Отче наш”: тропар мчц., Сл.: тр. прп., І нині: тр. свята. Відпуст середній з помин. свята, мчц. і прп.
Утреня: по “Бог Господь”: тропар свята, тр. мчц., Сл.: тр. прп., І нині: тр. свята. Катизми черг., сідал. свята. Пс. 50. Канон свята, мчц. та прп. Катавасія тільки по 3-ій, 6-ій, 8-ій та 9-ій піснях – ірмос канону прп. По 3-ій – конд.-ікос мчц. і прп., сідал. мчц., Сл.: сідал. прп., І нині: сідал. свята. По 6-ій – конд.-ікос свят; по 9-ій – Світил. мчц., Сл.: Світил. прп., І нині: Світил. свята. Стиховня свята. По “Отче наш”: тр. мчц., Сл.: тр. прп., І нині: тр. свята.
Часи: на всіх – тропар свята, Сл.: тр. мч. Конд: на 1-му і 6-му – свята, на 3-му – мчц.; на 9-му – прп.
Літургія: антифони: перші два – свята, третій – загальний з приспівом свята; “Прийдіте, поклонімся” з приспівом свята. Тропар свята, Сл., І нині: конд. свята; Прокімен, алилуя і причасний свята. Ап. – 1 Пт. 61 зач.; 4, 1-11. Єв. – Мр. 51 зач.; 11, 23-26. Замість “Достойно” – ірмос 9-ї пісні канону свята. Відпуст з помин. свята, мчц. і прп.

troparion_en.text:
(Podoben: “Joseph was amazed...”)
Your lamb Anysia calls out to You, O Jesus, in a loud voice: / “I love You, my Bridegroom, and in seeking You I endure suffering. / In baptism I was crucified so that I might reign in You, / and I died so that I might live with You. / Accept me as a pure sacrifice, / for I have offered myself in love.” / Through her prayers save our souls, since You are merciful.

kontakion_en.text: As a two-fold lamp you illumine the Church of Christ / with truly mystical radiance. / You brought forth a hundred-fold fruit through your contest, O Anysia; / and you shone forth in asceticism, O Melania. / You were both found worthy of the incorruptible life of the righteous.

oca_saints.name: Afterfeast of the Nativity of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ

troparion.text: Your Nativity, O Christ our God,  /  has shone to the world the Light of knowledge;  /  for by it, those who worshipped the stars / were taught by a star to adore You,  / the Sun of Righteousness,1  /  and to know You, the Dayspring2 from on High.  / O Lord, glory to You!

kontakion.text:
(Original Melody)
Today the Virgin gives birth to the Transcendent One,  /  and the earth offers a cave to the Unapproachable One!  / Angels with shepherds glorify Him.  /  The wise men journey with a star,  /  since for our sake the Pre-Eternal God was born as a young Child.3

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Joseph was amazed...”)
Your lamb Anysia calls out to You, O Jesus, in a loud voice: / “I love You, my Bridegroom, and in seeking You I endure suffering. / In baptism I was crucified so that I might reign in You, / and I died so that I might live with You. / Accept me as a pure sacrifice, / for I have offered myself in love.” / Through her prayers save our souls, since You are merciful.

kontakion.text: As a two-fold lamp you illumine the Church of Christ / with truly mystical radiance. / You brought forth a hundred-fold fruit through your contest, O Anysia; / and you shone forth in asceticism, O Melania. / You were both found worthy of the incorruptible life of the righteous.

oca_saints.name: Martyr Zoticus the Keeper of Orphans

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Zoticus, O Lord, / through his suffering has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Zoticus;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Apostle Timon the Deacon of the Seventy

troparion.text: Holy Apostle Timon, / entreat the merciful God, / to grant our souls forgiveness of transgressions.

Your holy martyr Philetairus, O Lord, / through his suffering has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Philetairus;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Theodora of Caesarea, in Cappadocia

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy mother Theodora, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy mother Theodora, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Monastic Martyr Gideon of Karakalou, Mount Athos

troparion.text: The image of God was truly preserved in you, O Father, / for you took up the Cross and followed Christ. / By so doing you taught us to disregard the flesh for it passes away / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal. / Therefore your spirit, venerable Gideon, rejoices with the angels.

kontakion.text: As a venerable and proven ascetic, as an honorable and renowned martyr,  / and as a model of the desert life, O most exalted Gideon, /  we sing hymns of praise to you,  / for you have trampled upon serpents.

oca_saints.name: Saint Macarius, Metropolitan of Moscow and All Russia

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Macarius, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

oca_saints.name: Opening of the relics of Saint Daniel of Pereyaslavl

troparion.text: From your youth, O blessed one, you devoted yourself entirely to the Lord,  / always obeying God and resisting the devil, you overcame sinful passions,  / thereby becoming a temple1 of God and you built a beautiful monastery for the glory of the Most Holy Trinity, / and for Christ's flock gathered in them, O God-pleaser, you departed to the eternal abodes, O Father Daniel.  / Entreat the tri-hypostatic God, one in essence, for the salvation of our souls.

kontakion.text: You appeared as an unfading Light, a bright luminary enlightening all with the purity of your life, O Father Daniel,   / because you were a model and a rule for monastics, a father of orphans, and benefactor of widows.   / Therefore, we your children cry out to you:   / “Rejoice, our joy and crown; rejoice, for you have a special right to address God;   / rejoice, great strengthener of our city.”
